sprint: Creator Signature Sky Challenge — Savor the Success
task: A real, user-reported UX bug: 'challenge screen, on correct tile choose screen transition is very fast, its not allowing user to savor the success, neither the voice is completed.' Root-caused, not guessed: js/magicCardUI.js's onCardTap() (the tap handler inside _renderSkyChallenge) fired opts.onSuccess() after a flat 900ms delay on a correct tap, regardless of how long the real recorded skySuccess voice line actually runs -- LumoVoice.durationMs('skySuccess') confirms it's 2429ms, so the screen was handing off roughly 1.5 seconds before the line finished, and the celebration state (the 'correct'/'fade' card classes plus the gatekeeper's 'celebrate' mood and '✨ There it is! Welcome back. ✨' message) was visible for barely a second -- exactly matching both halves of the report. Fixed by computing the hold from the clip's own real, measured duration instead of a guessed constant -- Math.max(900, LumoVoice.durationMs('skySuccess') + 900) -- mirroring the identical 'stretch the timer to fit the real recording' pattern js/gatewaySequence.js's playLines() already established elsewhere in this same feature; falls back to the original flat 900ms if LumoVoice is ever unavailable, matching the pre-existing defensive try/catch already wrapping the LumoVoice.play() call right above it. Disclosed scope decision: the wrong-tap path has the identical class of gap (skyWrong's own real clip runs 2847ms against a flat 1100ms regenerate-and-reshuffle delay, so a wrong guess's own voice line can get cut off by skyFresh starting right after) -- left untouched here since the user's report named the correct-tap transition specifically ('on correct tile choose'), not the wrong-tap flow; recorded in a code comment as a candidate for its own future fix if ever reported. Verified via a new Playwright test driving the real _renderSkyChallenge/beginCreatorSignature code path (not a reimplementation): identified the one real sky card deterministically by matching its rendered SVG star coordinates against the seeded card's own known pattern (rather than tap-and-see, which risks burning tries on decoys first), tapped it, and confirmed the real elapsed time from tap to onSuccess firing is now ~3.3 seconds, comfortably covering the full 2429ms voice line plus a genuine pause afterward -- versus the old, confirmed-too-short 900ms. Full regression across the existing sky-recognition-challenge/voice/scroll suites required widening several tests' own fixed post-tap wait timeouts to match the new, correctly longer hold -- the intended, disclosed consequence of this fix, not a regression (all three scenarios in the multi-batch challenge suite, the full Gateway-to-Studio-boot end-to-end path, and the voice-call-sequence/no-scroll suites all pass with the widened waits, identical pass/fail shape to before except for timing). Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this fix touches zero Builder files) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/magicCardUI.js.
